note: Rules fire on questionnaire v1 answers: a `question` trigger fires when the normalized answer is strictly below `belowNorm` (1 = anything short of full credit); a `category` trigger fires when the category score is strictly below `belowScore`. Items are ordered by priority (critical > high > medium > low), then by rule order in this file.

rules.title: Complete statutory audits of the last three fiscal years
rules.description: Certified accounts are the entry ticket to any listing: appoint or extend the statutory auditor's mandate and clear past reservations before the IPO calendar starts.

rules.title: Appoint a statutory auditor (commissaire aux comptes)
rules.description: A CAC is legally required well before the listing process; early appointment lets the first certified fiscal year start immediately.

rules.title: Prepare three years of financial statements
rules.description: Euronext admission requires up to three years of accounts; reconstruct or restate missing years with your accounting firm now, as this drives the whole timetable.

rules.title: Establish a formal board of directors
rules.description: Investors expect a functioning board with minutes and a meeting rhythm; set it up and run at least two quarters of real board practice before the roadshow.

rules.title: Consolidate and document the cap table
rules.description: Every instrument (shares, BSPCE, warrants, convertibles) must be reconciled and documented — the prospectus and the bookrunners will require a clean, auditable cap table.

rules.title: Set up an audit committee
rules.description: Required governance for listed companies; recruit at least one financially literate independent member and adopt a committee charter.

rules.title: Build a three-year business plan with financial projections
rules.description: The equity story rests on a credible, board-approved plan linking strategy to numbers; analysts will stress-test every assumption.

rules.title: Assess and plan the IFRS conversion
rules.description: Depending on the target market segment, IFRS may be mandatory; scope the conversion impact on revenue recognition, leases and share-based payments early.

rules.title: Implement a 12-month rolling cash forecast
rules.description: Working-capital statements in the prospectus require reliable cash visibility; institutionalise a monthly rolling forecast owned by the CFO.

rules.title: Strengthen the finance team for public-company reporting
rules.description: Quarterly closes, guidance and audits demand a staffed finance function; hire or formalise the CFO role and key controller positions.

rules.title: Document internal control procedures
rules.description: Write down the control environment (delegations, approvals, segregation of duties) — the AMF expects a description of internal control in the registration document.

rules.title: Establish the GDPR processing register
rules.description: Data-protection compliance is systematically reviewed in legal due diligence; build the register and remediate the gaps it reveals.

rules.title: Secure ownership of intellectual property
rules.description: Confirm every trademark, patent and code asset is owned by the listing entity (not founders or contractors); missing assignments are a classic deal-breaker found in due diligence.

rules.title: Set up a monthly management reporting pack
rules.description: A repeatable monthly pack (P&L, cash, KPIs, variance analysis) is the backbone of future quarterly publications; run it for several months to prove reliability.

rules.title: Convert to a listing-compatible legal form (SA)
rules.description: An SAS cannot list; plan the conversion to SA (or SCA) with counsel, including bylaws, governance bodies and shareholder approvals.

rules.title: Review the shareholders' agreement for listing compatibility
rules.description: Drag-along, lock-up and governance clauses often conflict with a free float; renegotiate or terminate the agreement as part of the IPO legal workstream.

rules.title: Build and maintain a company risk map
rules.description: The prospectus dedicates a full section to risk factors; a maintained risk map makes that section faster to draft and more credible.

rules.title: Define audited KPIs for each business line
rules.description: Pick the operating indicators you will guide the market on, define them precisely and reconcile them to the accounts so they survive auditor scrutiny.

rules.title: Automate management dashboards in a BI tool
rules.description: Manual spreadsheets do not scale to quarterly public reporting; automate the pack's data pipeline with clear ownership of each figure.

rules.title: Reduce top-client revenue concentration
rules.description: Concentration above 30% of revenue is a headline risk factor; build a commercial plan to diversify and document mitigating contracts.

rules.title: Improve forecast reliability
rules.description: Two consecutive budget cycles delivered within a narrow variance are the best proof your guidance can be trusted; instrument variance reviews monthly.

rules.title: Write the group accounting manual
rules.description: Documented closing procedures and accounting policies shorten audits and make the finance team resilient to turnover.

rules.title: Commission a market sizing study
rules.description: An independent TAM/SAM study underpins the growth narrative of the prospectus and the analyst presentations.

rules.title: Document competitive positioning against named peers
rules.description: Bankers will build the comparable universe anyway — arriving with your own peer analysis keeps you in control of the story.

rules.title: Take out directors' and officers' (D&O) insurance
rules.description: Board members of a listed company will require it; negotiate coverage before governance recruitment, not after.

rules.title: Prepare an investor-grade company presentation
rules.description: A standing equity-story deck accelerates every banker, analyst and anchor-investor conversation on the road to the IPO.

rules.title: Run a compliance remediation programme
rules.description: The compliance category scores below 50%: engage an IPO-readiness legal review covering audits, contracts, GDPR and litigation, and steer remediation as one programme.

rules.title: Build the reporting foundation end to end
rules.description: The reporting category scores below 50%: treat reporting as one project — data sources, KPIs, monthly pack, calendar — rather than isolated fixes.
